gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/221)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/221)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ كُلُوا الْبَصَلَ فَإِنَّ فِيهِ ثَلَاثَ خِصَالٍ يُطَيِّبُ النَّكْهَةَ وَ يَشُدُّ اللِّثَةَ وَ يَزِيدُ فِي الْمَاءِ وَ الْجِمَاعِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Bundar, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Ali Al hamdany, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Kaslaan, from Maysar Baya’a the Zaty, and he was his maternal uncle who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Eat the onion for therein are three qualities – It aromatises the breath, and strengthens the gums, and increases in the water and the copulation’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Bandar from his father from Muhammad ibn Ah al-Hamadaniy from al-Hassan ibn Ah al-Kaslan from Muyassir Bayya’ al-Zuttiy who was his maternal uncle has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘You must eat onion; it has three qualities; it is well flavored, brings firmness to one’s gums, increases one’s seeds and the ability to go to bed with one’s wife.’”
